short_description: Scope limited to workloads named in SOW; additional workloads = CR
APPSolve delivers OCI infra; not ongoing ops team without AMS SOW
Client holds OCI tenancy agreement directly with Oracle
Default region: Oracle South Africa (Johannesburg); multi-region requires explicit SOW
Single OCI tenancy assumed; multiple tenancies separately scoped
Client must provide named technical contacts with approval authority
OCI delivery based on Oracle Well-Architected Framework; deviations documented
Oracle SaaS (Fusion HCM/ERP) is Oracle-operated; OCI work limited to OIC/APEX/hybrid
Standard Landing Zone: compartments + IAM + tagging + budget alerts; customisation separately scoped
Three standard compartments: Production / Non-Production / Shared Services
Greenfield tenancy assumed; existing tenancy requires paid audit first
Landing Zone delivered as Terraform/ORM IaC; client receives artefacts at close
Standard tagging: environment / cost centre / project / owner; custom tags separately scoped
Budget alert thresholds set from client-provided budget data; APPSolve does not define budgets
No multi-cloud federation included (Azure Arc / AWS Control Tower)
No automatic resource lifecycle management; optional add-on if required
ORM used for Terraform execution; custom pipeline separately scoped
Standard Landing Zone Runbook included; bespoke procedure manuals are out of scope
Oracle Identity Domains used (not legacy IDCS); IDCS migration separately scoped
Standard IAM groups: tenancy/compartment/network/security/DBA admins + read-only auditors
IdP federation (Entra ID / Okta / ADFS) in scope where stated in SOW; requires client identity team
Single Identity Domain assumed; multiple domains separately scoped and may require additional Oracle licensing
PAM tooling integration (CyberArk / BeyondTrust) not included; specialist security scope
APPSolve automation service accounts are least-privilege; handed over at project close
Client must designate at least one internal OCI tenancy administrator
MFA enforced by default for all OCI Console users; disabling requires written client approval
Least-privilege IAM policies; no wildcard policies in production
Break-Glass access procedures documented in Runbook; APPSolve does not hold post-project credentials
One VCN per major environment (Production / Non-Production); additional VCNs separately scoped
VCN CIDR assigned by client network team; CIDR conflicts are client responsibility
IPSec VPN is default on-premise connectivity; FastConnect requires separate commercial decision
Client network team must provide VPN gateway IP / pre-shared keys / routing before configuration
Hub-and-Spoke VCN topology is standard; flat single-VCN available for simple engagements
Service Gateway enabled by default; Internet Gateway only where explicitly required
OCI Private DNS for internal; external DNS managed by client DNS provider
NSGs and Security Lists restrict traffic between tiers; client approves rules before deployment
OCI Network Firewall (DPI / IPS) is additional licensed OCI service; separately scoped
Flexible Load Balancer included where named in SOW; application-specific tuning included
OCI Bastion Service for all admin access; no direct public IP to production compute
Third-party network appliances (Palo Alto / Fortinet) out of scope; OCI-native only
CIS OCI Foundations Benchmark Level 1 as baseline; higher standards separately scoped
Cloud Guard enabled and configured; post-handover remediation is client's responsibility
Security Zones applied to Production compartment; disabling requires CISO written approval
Oracle-managed encryption keys by default; CMEK via OCI Vault requires explicit SOW inclusion
TLS enforced for all data in transit; plaintext channels not configured in production
Audit logs retained 90 days by default; extended retention requires Object Storage export
Penetration testing out of APPSolve scope; procured from specialist vendor
Vulnerability scanning is client operational responsibility post-handover
Data residency maintained within contracted OCI region; cross-border requires explicit client approval
Security Advisor run during engagement; report provided at handover; accepted findings documented
Instance sizing from client-provided or Oracle-documented specifications; APPSolve does not independently validate
Standard shape: VM.Standard.E4.Flex or VM.Standard3.Flex unless application requires otherwise
Oracle Linux as default OS; non-Oracle OS licensing is client responsibility
Oracle baseline OS hardening; CIS/STIG/NIST hardening separately scoped
All compute provisioned via Terraform/ORM; no manual console provisioning for production
Auto-scaling configured where explicitly required; not assumed by default
HA using Availability Domain / Fault Domain placement per application requirements
Production boot volume backups included; non-production backups optional
Object Storage for DB backups / exports / audit log archiving / static content
All Object Storage buckets private by default; public buckets require written client approval
Block Volume sizing from client-provided requirements; capacity planning is client responsibility
Block Volume performance tier (Balanced/Higher/Ultra High) selected per application I/O requirements
File Storage Service only where shared file system required; not assumed by default
Object Storage lifecycle: Standard 30 days → Infrequent Access 60 days → Archive; client may override
No cross-region Object Storage replication by default; included only where DR design requires it
Oracle-managed storage encryption by default; CMEK via OCI Vault must be explicitly included
VM DB System as standard; Exadata / ADB requires specific design decision
BYOL default where client holds existing Oracle DB licences; LI otherwise
DB version certified for the application; non-certified version upgrade is risk-accepted variance
Data Guard as standard HA mechanism; RAC/Active DG requires explicit design and licensing
Automated RMAN backups enabled for production; 7-day default retention
Automated backups do not replace Data Pump / application-level exports; separate scope
DB patching is client operational responsibility post-handover; runbook provided
DBA services (tuning / schema / AWR) not in OCI infra scope; in ERP/HCM pack scope
Autonomous Database included where solution requires ADB services; schema design not included
Cross-region Data Guard (for DR) separately scoped; single-region HA does not include cross-region replication
GoldenGate on OCI is separate licensed service; not assumed in standard delivery
DB performance benchmarking / stress testing not included; separate optional activity
OIC provisioned as managed PaaS; OCI network access in scope; integration flows in OIC pack
APEX managed service default; self-managed APEX on compute requires explicit design decision
WebLogic on OCI via Marketplace image; post-handover patching is client responsibility
SOA on OCI via Marketplace image; no SOA re-architecture in lift-and-shift
OAC provisioning and VCN access in scope; OAC content development in Analytics pack
Oracle Marketplace images as deployment baseline; custom software installation separately scoped
Middleware HA (WebLogic cluster / SOA cluster) where required by design; single-node default for non-prod
Third-party middleware (IBM MQ / Kafka / MuleSoft) out of OCI pack scope
OCI integration scope = network connectivity only; integration logic in Integration pack
API Gateway provisioned where managed API exposure required; API design not in OCI scope
OCI Functions (serverless) provisioned where design requires; function code from app team
Oracle Streaming Service provisioned where event streaming required; producers/consumers not in OCI scope
B2B integration via OIC B2B Service; SFTP on compute for legacy file-based integrations
OCI Email Delivery configured where required; client verifies sender domain via DNS
OCI Notifications/Events configured for operational monitoring; app-level event subscriptions separate
Third-party iPaaS (Boomi / Informatica / Talend) on OCI out of OCI pack scope
Backup scope: Block Volume + Boot Volume + DB System Automatic Backups; app-level backups in app pack
Standard backup schedule: daily incremental (30-day retention) + weekly full (60-day retention)
RMAN backup to Object Storage; window agreed with client and documented in runbook
One restore test during engagement; ongoing testing is client responsibility post-handover
No cross-region backup replication by default; included only where DR design requires it
Third-party backup tools (Veeam / Commvault) out of scope; OCI-native only
Backup alerting via OCI Notifications to client-designated emails; PagerDuty integration not included
Archive tier for long-term retention; 1–4 hour retrieval time documented in runbook
DR only where explicitly stated in SOW; no DR assumed by default
Standard DR offering: Pilot Light (minimal state in secondary region; activated on disaster declaration)
Active-Active / warm standby DR is premium separately priced; typically doubles OCI cost
RTO/RPO defined by client; APPSolve designs to targets but does not guarantee achievement
One DR failover test included; one remediation cycle included; additional cycles are CR
Data Guard cross-region for DB DR; OCI Full Stack DR or Pilot Light for app tier
No DR for third-party applications hosted on OCI unless in project scope with OCI-native mechanism
DR runbook is standard deliverable: declaration criteria / activation steps / recovery time / failback
Network DR (routing / DNS failover) included in DR design; DNS TTL changes are client DNS team responsibility
OCI Full Stack DR Service used for supported resource types; manual runbooks for unsupported types
Standard dashboards: compute / database / network / storage utilisation; custom dashboards out of scope
OCI Logging enabled for all services; Logging Analytics requires client subscription
Standard alert conditions: CPU >90% / Memory >85% / Disk >80% / DB unavailability / backup failure
Third-party monitoring integration (Splunk / Dynatrace / Datadog) not included; optional if explicitly in SOW
Application Performance Monitoring (APM) not included; requires separate scope and agent instrumentation
OCI Operations Insights for DB performance is optional; not assumed by default
Monitoring Runbook delivered at close: dashboards / alerts / response procedures / escalation contacts
Uptime SLA reporting not in project scope; included only under AMS Managed OCI SOW
Project mode delivery; operational responsibility transfers at close; AMS SOW for ongoing ops
OS Management Hub enabled for Oracle Linux patching; post-close patch execution is client responsibility
Oracle Cloud Lift not assumed; APPSolve scopes and prices work independently
OCI Governance rules (Quotas / Budget Alerts / Policies) configured; post-close quota management is client
Post-handover changes follow client's IT change management process; project-mode changes follow CR framework
Maintenance windows for patching agreed before handover; production changes coordinated with client IT
Standard operational handover training included; advanced OCI admin training separately scoped
Incident management uses client's ITSM tooling post-handover; AMS SOW includes ITSM integration
OCI infrastructure costs are client's financial responsibility; billed by Oracle directly
Indicative OCI cost estimates for budgeting only; not contractual commitments
Cost optimisation recommendations provided as standard; implementation post-delivery is client responsibility
Reserved Instances recommended where appropriate; purchasing is client's commercial decision with Oracle
Non-production auto-shutdown configurable on request; client defines uptime schedule
Network egress costs highlighted in design review; actual costs are client responsibility
FinOps advisory (showback / chargeback / unit cost) not included; separate optional engagement
OCI Cost Analysis and Budgets dashboards configured as part of standard Landing Zone
Migration Plan mandatory deliverable for all OCI migrations; defines approach / sequence / rollback criteria
Supported approaches: Lift-and-Shift / ZDM database migration / Marketplace image re-platform
Zero Downtime Migration preferred for Oracle DB; alternative approach documented if ZDM not viable
Pre-migration assessment mandatory: inventory / dependency mapping / network / cutover risk
Cutover is planned event with agreed window; extended cutover >48h requires resourcing agreement
Rollback maintained during cutover window; rollback after acceptance sign-off is new project activity
Source environment decommissioning is client responsibility; APPSolve provides checklist only
Data volumes >10TB require data transfer strategy review (FastConnect / Data Transfer Appliance / internet)
applies_to: OCI migration engagements with large data

short_description: Parallel run recommended during migration testing; parallel run costs are client responsibility
Application compatibility validation is client / application vendor responsibility; APPSolve ensures infra meets spec
Third-party application migration out of scope; APPSolve migrates Oracle workloads named in SOW only
Post-migration performance validation included; issues within 30 days = defect; after 30 days = operational
OCI infra designed to Oracle documented sizing guidelines; no independent performance modelling
Performance testing (load / stress / soak) not included; separate scope; production tests need maintenance window
Auto-scaling triggers based on CPU/memory metrics; app-level scale-out validation is app team responsibility
VPN bandwidth limitation highlighted in design review; FastConnect upgrade is separate commercial item
Block Volume IOPS from client-provided requirements; no on-premise I/O profiling without explicit scope
DB performance subject to shape and I/O tier; incorrect sizing info from client is not a delivery defect
No performance guarantees (SLA-backed) in project delivery; performance SLAs are AMS Managed OCI only
Right-sizing recommendations provided at close and at 30-day post-go-live review; post-review actions are client operational
Oracle Cloud support is client's direct Oracle relationship; Premier Support recommended for production
APPSolve project delivery support until acceptance sign-off; post-acceptance requires AMS SOW
AMS OCI support covers OCI services named in AMS SOW only; expansion requires SOW amendment
OCI platform outages escalated to Oracle by APPSolve; Oracle SLA governs resolution
OCI tenancy administration included as standard service request type in AMS SOW
Security incident response follows APPSolve procedures; 24x7 SOC requires separately scoped on-call retainer
OCI cost spike investigation included as standard service request in Managed OCI AMS SOW
APPSolve OCI support team holds current OCI certifications (min: Architect Associate; senior: Architect Professional)
FastConnect provisioning requires client to contract FastConnect partner; lead time 6–12 weeks
Third-party SaaS integration = OCI network connectivity only; integration logic in Integration pack
OCI Marketplace ISV software provisioned by APPSolve; ISV support is client/ISV relationship
CDN (OCI/Akamai) included only where explicitly required; not assumed by default
OCI Government regions out of standard pack scope; requires separate engagement design
Product-specific OCI assumptions belong in respective product packs; OCI pack governs shared infrastructure layer
Data residency maintained within contracted OCI region; cross-border compliance is client legal responsibility
Oracle Customer Connect available to clients with Oracle support; does not replace paid Oracle support
ISV licence compliance on OCI is client responsibility; APPSolve provisions to ISV spec
EBS on OCI requires active EBS software support with Oracle; assumed held by client
